ranked.text:
Most people think the future of AI is constrained by how much energy we can generate. I think it's constrained by how stupidly we spend the energy we already have—and I'm building Aurelius to fix that. The bottleneck isn't power. It's that no one optimizes the fleet's trajectory across decisions, so waste compounds. In a simulated replay of ~1.5M public production requests, our scheduler delivered 724% more goodput per dollar than the baseline. Send me the one-pager. Best,
Beckett

I'm Beckett, 17, starting Princeton in the fall but building Aurelius—a system that forecasts power constraints and picks the best scheduling path for AI fleets over time, not just the next placement.

The bottleneck isn't power. It's that no one optimizes the fleet's trajectory across decisions, so waste compounds.

Simulated replay of ~1.5M public requests showed 724% more goodput per dollar than the production scheduler.

Send me the one-pager.

Best,
Beckett

result.honesty: best-supported candidate under the current assumptions; no reliable distinction between finalists (uncalibrated evaluator — real outreach outcomes, not more simulation, would sharpen this)
